meta.setting: Small print shop and muddy street in French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in rumor, public accusation, and town pressure

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-town commerce, courthouse streets, and public town texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining community roads, settlement pressure, and public rumor context.

image_prompt.subject: two ink-stained press boys running fresh newspaper sheets from a small print shop into a muddy street as adults turn to stare
image_prompt.scene: windy afternoon; press clatter visible inside, stacks of unreadable papers, horse splashing mud nearby, courthouse steps beyond, and a sheet nearly lost in the wheel rut
image_prompt.composition: square motion scene with papers flying as abstract shapes, boys cutting across the town line, and adult attention turning like a fuse catching
image_prompt.camera: low street-level chase angle, game-like freeze of movement with early-HD silhouettes
material_cues: newsprint sheets with no readable text
horse legs cutting through the street edge
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make them sharp, not adorable; they know the headline before the sheriff does.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine street motion scene
render_traits: slightly simplified boys, adults, horse, and paper silhouettes
modest texture resolution on paper, mud, wood, cloth, and ink
hard ambient occlusion under feet, doorframe, horse legs, and paper stacks
windy afternoon light with mud shine
rumor-in-motion composition rather than cute newspaper poster
style_modifiers.palette: newsprint gray, ink black, mud brown, faded shirt blue, courthouse stone gray, horsehide brown, and windy sky white.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable headlines, letters, labels, signs, or text. News sheets must show only blurred columns and dark shapes.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The press boys, print shop, papers, street, horse, and staring adults are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to an 1899 print-shop street.
The image shows news as fast social pressure without readable text.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
